item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I created an account to *finally* fix the typo, since it's one of my favorite SCP's. Only took 6 years to fix the typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to its non-physical nature, SCP-2113-01 has in the past shown capable of moving through physical barriers to reach its targets. It has never been seen to travel more than 100 metres from SCP-2113-01.

Is that last 2113-01 just meant to be 2113? If not it seems to say that it can't move 100 meters from itself.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I don't see why that part of the page in particular would have the cross-link… people who look at the link might think it has something to do with the Liopleurodon, when SCP-3637 is infact an SCP page on a Maiasaura fossil.
Personally i'd just have an addendum akin to what some other SCPs have that pertains to the similarities of the SCPs.

item.title: Re: A theory
item.content:encoded: I stand by these remarks.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

There are many similarities between this skip and SCP-3637, primarily the fact that they're both dinosaurs that come back from the dead and break my heart.

Editing this:

Experiences are relatively consistent between victims, with a majority reporting that they found themselves existing as a large animal (presumably Liopleurodon ferox) within a warm aquatic environment.

To this:

Experiences are relatively consistent between victims, with a majority reporting that they found themselves existing as a large animal (presumably Liopleurodon ferox) within a warm aquatic environment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is classic-SCP good. Concise as hell and it leaves the imagination bubbling and wanting more. It's fleshed out just enough to feel lived in. The diversion into more poetic language in the addendum account is pleasing and evocative without being distracting or show-offy. I'm not saying it's earth shaking, but damn, this is how it's done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But when I was going to upvote, it had +71, and the second I upvoted it turned to +66? That doesn't seem right.

Wikidot recently removed the votes from deleted accounts on our site. The counter, unfortuantly, only gets updated when someone upvotes or downvotes an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most likely your computer registering votes from deleted users that were removed when we had to deal with a vote spamming troll.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I really enjoyed this. The depersonalization was unnexpected and grabbed my curiosity, and really paid off. +1

But when I was going to upvote, it had +71, and the second I upvoted it turned to +66? That doesn't seem right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The typos are still there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCPs that force historical experience have been done before. There really isn't anything that feels mysterious about this one either: somehow it was found, it came into Foundation possession, we learned everything about it, and then we re-buried it(?) No containment measures have been undertaken to secure the ghost, which feels a little lax for Foundation procedure, and the effects are just really predictable.

There was one thing I did find a little creepy/interesting was the account of the fossilization experience. "Hardening"/"hard numb" was a bit interesting, as one rarely considers what fossilization would feel like, and the ambiguous descriptions of scraping and deserts definitely caught my eye. It might be better if the SCP were centered around this theme.

item.title: A theory
item.content:encoded:
What if liopleurodon were sentient and had an advanced civilization like ours, and this is what's left of their Internet? Imagine what the chatrooms would be like:
"Oh, hai. Im a 166,000,000 year-old liopleurodon ferox, but everyone says im like, relly mature for my age, im female, bi, and currently in a underground cell at Bio-Site 66 of the SPC Foundation (lol). My interests include hunting, swimming, beaching myself, and haunting my own fossilized remains #yolodontapply lol. A/S/S/L1?

Footnotes
1. Age/Species/Sex/Location

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite an interesting SCIP. I never thought anyone would make something like this.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. It depends what the author is going for. In monster movies or books it's best to leave the monster unrevealed, because nothing is scarier than the unknown or unknowable. That said, it if's not meant to be horroresque, a description would help some.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoy the skip, but I feel that there should be a more substantial description of -01's physical appearance. One can infer that it might look similar to a complete skeleton/living counterpart of 2113 from what description there is, but I believe the quality of the article could be improved if you gave the reader more to go off of regarding -01.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is there any relevance to the shape of SCP-2113-1? Or is it just a kind of generic ghost shape?

Either way this is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a size estimation. I'm almost happy with this one, but too think there's something missing. I'll come back in a month to see if i can comb through and add or fix anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a magical Liopleurodon, Charlie.

I dunno. For one thing, I'd like some more description of -01, at least as far as size goes. The language in the addendum is very odd. And I think I'm getting some feedback from having just read 2077, because I'm going, "Two depersonalization skips in one day? That's too much!" But that's hardly your fault.

I definitely feel this is missing something, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for catching the typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor baby. I might write a tale about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure that was what was meant, yes.

the ghost of a Jurassic aquatic predator. My upvote, sir - use it in good health.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea of the ghost of a prehistoric animal that makes people experience its life. On the other hand, why would that researcher in particular hallucinate its death and the millions of years following it, and why would he have a heart attack forty hours afterwards?

Also, it doesn't make much sense that SCP-2113-01 can't go far from SCP-2113-01. Did you mean that it can't go far from SCP-2113?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you to Photosynthetic, DrMann, TheDeadlyMoose, Soulless, and Xiao for helping me out and/or looking this over.
